title: scrophularia alata subsp. ehrahrtii
intro: Scrophularia alata subsp. ehrahrtii, also known as the Wallflower figwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its use as an anti-inflammatory agent, due to the presence of iridoid glycosides. The health benefits of Scrophularia alata subsp. ehrahrtii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and skin irritations.
